abstract.para: The &cockpit; Web interface supports modularity through plug-in applications. This section describes how to view, navigate and remove these applications using the Applications page.

topic.para: &cockpit; applications (or modules) extend the functionality of the Web interface. Adding an application creates a new entry in the sidebar, allowing you to manage complex system services—such as firewalls, virtual machines or SELinux—through a visual interface rather than the command line.
Commonly installed modules include: Networking, Software Updates (Packages), Repositories and SELinux.

section.title: Viewing and navigating applications
section.para: You can access your installed modules by clicking the Applications tab in the left-hand navigation pane and clicking the application's name to switch to its dedicated management tab. To uninstall a module, locate the application in the list and click the Remove button.

section.title: Troubleshooting missing applications
section.para: If the Applications page is empty, or if a newly installed module does not appear in the list, you may need to refresh the &cockpit; service.
Restart the socket: Run the following command in your terminal:
Check for service failures: If a specific service fails to start, the Applications page will display the relevant systemd error logs directly on the screen to assist with debugging.
